title: Sea salt texturising hair spray
subtitle: A light spray with sea salt, aloe vera juice, and lavender for natural texture and hold
excerpt: Sea salt sprays add texture and light hold by drawing out moisture slightly and encouraging the hair's natural wave pattern. This version combines fine sea salt with aloe vera juice for a small amount of definition and isopropyl alcohol to keep the blend shelf-stable. A few spritzes on damp or dry hair and scrunch. Yield is approximately 200 ml in a spray bottle.
sourceNotes: Sea salt sprays: the salt draws hygroscopic moisture from the hair shaft, temporarily altering the texture and encouraging curl and wave definition. Fine sea salt at 2% of water weight is within the range that gives texture without making the hair crunchy or brittle. Aloe vera juice as a light hold agent: the mucilaginous polysaccharides in aloe vera form a flexible film on the hair shaft. Isopropyl alcohol at 7% acts as a preservative and emulsifier for the essential oil. Lavender EO: fragrance only at this dilution; rinse-off product so no leave-on concentration concern.

recipe.yieldDescription: Approximately 200 ml in a spray bottle
recipe.batchNotes: Double or triple the recipe and store in a larger bottle. Shake before each use.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Ready to use immediately. Label with the date made. Shake before use as salt can settle.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 3 months with the isopropyl alcohol acting as a preservative. Discard if the spray becomes cloudy, develops sediment that doesn't disperse on shaking, or smells off.

glossaryTerms.definition: A substance that helps water and oil combine into a stable mixture. In this spray, isopropyl alcohol partially emulsifies the essential oil into the water phase and also acts as a preservative. Without it, the oil would float as a separate layer.

content.text: Salt sprays work by drawing moisture out of the outer layer of the hair shaft, temporarily swelling the cuticle and encouraging the hair to group into waves or curls. Fine sea salt at 2% of the water weight gives texture without making the hair stiff or crunchy. Aloe vera juice adds a light flexible hold from its natural polysaccharides. Isopropyl alcohol at 7% acts as an

for the essential oil and as a preservative for the water-containing blend. This spray suits wavy and curly hair types; those with very dry or fine hair may find the salt effect drying on frequent use.

items.prepNote: Warm slightly to dissolve the sea salt
Cosmetic-grade aloe vera juice; adds light hold
90%+ grade; acts as preservative and emulsifier for the essential oil
2% of water weight; fine sea salt dissolves cleanly
Fragrance; add to the alcohol first and mix before combining with water

content.text: Gently warm the distilled water. Add the sea salt and stir until fully dissolved; do not heat above 50°C. Allow to cool to room temperature. In a small bowl or directly in the spray bottle, combine the isopropyl alcohol with the lavender essential oil and mix briefly. Add the aloe vera juice, then the cooled salt water. Stir or gently swirl to combine.
Pour into the spray bottle. Seal and label with the batch date and a shake instruction. Shake before each use to redistribute the salt and any essential oil that has separated.

Shake the bottle. Spritz onto damp or dry hair from about 20 cm away. Scrunch upwards with your hands to encourage waves. Allow to air-dry or diffuse. For best results, apply to towel-dried hair after washing. Use every 2 to 3 days rather than daily to avoid salt build-up.

Store at room temperature away from direct sunlight. With the isopropyl alcohol acting as a preservative, the spray keeps for about 3 months. Shake before use. Discard if the spray develops an off smell or visible cloudiness that does not clear on shaking.
